item.title: News
item.description: Stay up to date on new cybersecurity solutions, as well as BI.ZONE news and events

item.title: BI.ZONE to contribute to MENA ISC
item.description: We will host a panel discussion with international experts, hold several workshops, open the Cyber Polygon 2024 training, and present an overview of the trends in the GCC cyber threat landscape

item.title: OFFZONE 2024 attracts record attendance
item.description: In the two days of the conference, 124 experts spoke to a crowd of 4,000 people. The event was supported by 50 partners, including 9 media outlets and 17 community groups

item.title: BI.ZONE EDR expands capabilities to better assess endpoint configuration
item.description: The updated BI.ZONE EDR version received a new Safety Recommendations module. It is available on all operating systems and allows you to evaluate OS and software configuration on endpoints, identify their vulnerabilities and accounts with weak passwords

item.title: Undercover attack: nearly 85% of phishing emails are disguised as financial and government documents
item.description: Our research shows that 68% of attacks on companies in Russia and other CIS countries last year began with phishing emails. In the first six months of 2024, this share reached 76%

item.title: BI.ZONE Threat Intelligence offers a new analytical tool
item.description: The portal's latest version features an all-in-one tool based on the MITRE ATT&CK matrix

item.title: Participants from over 20 countries now listed for Cyber Polygon 2024 training
item.description: Teams from Colombia, France, Mexico, Switzerland, the UK, the USA, and other countries have already signed up for the training exercise

item.title: Sapphire Werewolf polishes open‑source stealer to spy on Russian organizations
item.description: Since March 2024, a new criminal group Sapphire Wolf has initiated more than 300 attacks on Russian organizations. The threat actor steals sensitive data using a modified stealer. The targeted industries include education, IT, defense, and aerospace engineering

item.title: Registration for Cyber Polygon 2024 now open
item.description: The Cyber Polygon 2024 international training is open for registration. The exercise will run online on September 10–11 during the MENA ISC in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia

item.title: BI.ZONE WAF counters new vulnerability in Confluence
item.description: In late May, Atlassian disclosed a new severe vulnerability that exists in multiple versions of their Confluence Data Center and Server. BI.ZONE WAF specialists quickly responded with a rule for detecting illegitimate activity and preventing the exploitation of the vulnerability

item.title: Presenting on-prem BI.ZONE EDR endpoint security solution
item.description: The BI.ZONE EDR on-prem version contains all the features that proved effective in the BI.ZONE TDR (SOC/MDR). The agents for Linux, Windows, and macOS have also got a number of updates

item.title: Haste makes waste: Scaly Wolf’s attacks fail over a major blunder
item.description: Instead of the White Snake stealer, the group's new loader installed a legitimate file on compromised devices

item.title: BI.ZONE WAF responds to multiple Formidator WordPress plug‑in vulnerabilities
item.description: Three critical vulnerabilities have been identified in the Formidator plug-in of the WordPress Content Management System. BI.ZONE WAF and security assessment teams have analyzed the flaws and developed rules to prevent the exploitation of vulnerabilities

item.title: New BI.ZONE CESP protects against quishing, takes into account employee cyber awareness levels
item.description: We have updated our email protection service to defend organizations from more malicious scams

item.title: BI.ZONE CPT updated to better handle threats to web applications
item.description: The updated solution enables users to identify all vulnerable web resources in one click and take protective action

item.title: OFFZONE 2025: dates and venue confirmed
item.description: The sixth international conference on practical cybersecurity OFFZONE will run on August 21–22 in Moscow's GOELRO

item.title: Matter of trust: 400 companies breached through a legitimate tool
item.description: The adversaries compromised at least 400 organizations in Russia and other CIS countries through the legitimate remote administration tool NetSupport

item.title: New year, new stealer: January starts with a large‑scale campaign targeting Russian companies
item.description: Adversaries employ NOVA stealer, a modified variant of SnakeLogger. The extracted credentials can be used by any cluster for targeted attacks against the compromised companies

item.title: Nearly impossible to detect: GuLoader weaponized against Russian organizations
item.description: The loader ingeniously bypasses security solutions and execution environments, including virtual machines and sandboxes

item.title: Over 65% of host vulnerabilities stem from misconfigurations
item.description: Password policy violations account for the largest share of misconfigurations. Unsafe password policies are behind 35% of this year's most severe cybersecurity incidents

item.title: Espionage attacks on the rise with more devastating consequences
item.description: The share of espionage attacks on Russian organizations has increased by 6% year-on-year. Some threat actors go beyond covert data collection to the disruption of compromised IT infrastructures

item.title: Cyber Polygon 2024 highlights
item.description: Cyber Polygon 2024 was held within MENA ISC, the region's leading cybersecurity conference. This year, over 300 organizations from 65 countries joined the training

item.title: RATs and stealers are most favored by adversaries attacking Russian business
item.description: Remote access trojans (RATs), the type of malware that enables threat actors to run commands on compromised devices remotely, are used in more than half of all attacks on Russian organizations

item.title: Core Werewolf bolsters attacks against Russia’s defense industry and critical infrastructure
item.description: In September, the cluster started using a new internally crafted tool. The adversaries also experiment with malware delivery methods—in addition to phishing emails, they reach out to potential victims in instant messengers

item.title: Hackers favor uncommon pentest tools over malware
item.description: By opting for lesser known tools, adversaries increase their chances of staying invisible in a compromised IT infrastructure

item.title: CVE-2024-7965 Google Chrome vulnerability affects Android smartphones and macOS laptops
item.description: We have analyzed a critical vulnerability in Google Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine. Our research has revealed that the vulnerability threatens the users of Android-powered smartphones and certain macOS models

item.title: More than 300 organizations from 65 countries participated in Cyber Polygon 2024
item.description: This year's Cyber Polygon technical training was held during the MENA ISC conference in Saudi Arabia, the largest cybersecurity conference in the region

item.title: Key trends in GCC cyber threat landscape to be presented at MENA ISC 2024
item.description: 8 in 10 of the most active threat actors attack for espionage, and only 2 in 10 attack for ransom. These and other key trends in the cyber threat landscape of the GCC region will be presented at the Cyber Polygon booth during MENA ISC 2024

item.title: Hackers increasingly ignore restrictions of commercial malware developers
item.description: The attackers disable the modules that prevent the malware from being deployed in the Commonwealth of Independent States. Modified, restriction-free malware versions are sold on underground forums and Telegram channels

item.title: We uncover vulnerability chain in Oracle VirtualBox
item.description: CVE-2025-62592 and CVE-2025-61760 rated 6.0 and 7.5 (CVSS 3.1), accordingly
